assessment.title: M2 Types of Evidence and Fingerprint Analysis Assessment

material.mattext: Which of the following is an identified piece of physical evidence?
In a court case, a piece of individualized physical evidence is more valuable than a piece of identified physical evidence because it can be
grouped into a class of items having similar properties
classified into various categories having different traits
considered to be circumstantial depending on the case
Which of the following pieces of physical evidence would most likely help convince a jury that a suspect is guilty?
A defense lawyer would likely try to argue that any piece of physical evidence presented by the prosecution is

Scenario #1: When the shoes seized from a break-and-enter suspect were examined, a small piece of glass was found embedded in one sole. Analysis determined that the unique piece of glass came directly from the crime scene.

Type of physical evidence =

Scenario #2:A victim's body surfaced nine months after being dumped into a lake. The body had been tied to cement blocks with a torn bed sheet. Forensic scientists were asked to determine if a certain hammer caused the unique markings on the skull. The autopsy determined that the victim had been struck with a ball peen hammer.

Type of physical evidence =

Scenario #3: A plaster cast of a faint shoe print was taken from the scene of a burglary. It was compared to the tread of a suspect's shoe. The shoe print found at the scene appeared to match the tread of the suspect's right shoe.

Type of physical evidence =

Scenario #4: Plaster casts were taken of a faint shoe print at a burglary scene and of a suspect's shoe. The tread of the shoe print found at the scene appeared to match the suspect's right shoe. Even the location of a staple stuck in the tread of the suspect's shoe matched the impression in the shoe print cast.

Type of physical evidence =

Scenario #5: The body of a hit-and-run victim was found next to a major highway. A suspect vehicle was located and examined. An impression of a watch wristband found on the car's bumper matched the hit-and-run victim's watch wristband.

Type of physical evidence =
could be considered both identified and individualized

Scenario #6: A young boy was kidnapped and his parents were sent a stenciled ransom note. A stencil was later found at a suspect's home; it matched the stenciled letters on the ransom note.

Type of physical evidence =
The most common type of fingerprint ridge pattern is the
Which of the following is an example of physical fingerprint evidence?
Where are fingerprint ridges located on the skin of the hand?
The reason latent fingerprints must be enhanced is they
are not visible to the human eye
The type of lifting powder used by a forensic expert is often dependent upon
What is used to lift a fingerprint after lifting powder has been applied?
The iodine used in the iodine fumigation process is a
The ion in sweat in a fingerprint to which iodide is attracted is
What color is the sodium iodide that forms during iodine fumigation?
Cyanoacrylate is found in a commercial product known as
To what substance found in a fingerprint will cyanoacrylate readily adhere?
During the fumigation process, what must be present in the fumigation chamber in addition to cyanoacrylate?
What color does cyanoacrylate leave upon a fingerprint impression during fumigation?
An advantage of cyanoacrylate fumigation is that fingerprints exposed are
Identify the type of fingerprint enhancement technique that should be used to expose fingerprints found on a drinking glass or a window?
What type of fingerprint enhancement technique should be used to expose fingerprints found on a knife handle?
What type of fingerprint enhancement technique should be used to expose fingerprints found on a Five-dollar bill?
